title: 10 best herbal decoctions for jaw clenching
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for jaw clenching are a natural remedy that combines the therapeutic properties of herbs to alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, including jaw clenching. By steeping herbs like lavender, chamomile, and peppermint in hot water, these decoctions release calming compounds that soothe tense muscles, reduce anxiety, and relax the jaw. For example, drinking a warm cup of chamomile tea before bed can help individuals with bruxism sleep more soundly without grinding their teeth. This natural remedy can greatly improve daily life by reducing discomfort, promoting relaxation, and enhancing overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava decoctions helps with jaw clenching because its unique blend of compounds, particularly kavalactones, interacts with the brain's neurotransmitters to calm the nervous system. This relaxation response reduces muscle tension in the jaw, alleviating symptoms of bruxism and teeth grinding. As the body releases its hold on the jaw, it becomes easier to relax and release tension, promoting a sense of calm and reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching episodes. With regular use, kava decoctions can help individuals break the habit of chronic jaw clenching and enjoy a healthier, more relaxed smile.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, a group of compounds found in kava roots, have been shown to relax muscles, including those involved in jaw clenching, reducing tension and pain.
Anxiolytic properties: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects of kava decoctions can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related jaw clenching, which is a common underlying cause of bruxism.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava decoctions have been found to have muscle relaxant effects, helping to reduce muscle spasms and tension in the jaw, face, and neck that contribute to clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in kavalactones, which have been traditionally used to relax muscle tension and alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Containing a higher concentration of kavalactones than roots, rhyzomes are often preferred for their potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh kava roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the kava roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine 1 tablespoon of chopped kava root with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container to create a decoction.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the herbal decoction in an airtight glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 1/2 cup three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the muscles of the face, particularly the masseter and temporalis muscles that are responsible for jaw movement. The flavonoids and saponins present in maypop flowers and leaves help to relax these muscles, reducing tension and promoting relaxation. This can be especially beneficial for individuals who grind their teeth or clench their jaw due to stress or anxiety, as it helps to release the tension and promote a more relaxed facial posture.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Maypop decoctions have been shown to relax muscle spasms, including those in the jaw, which helps to reduce tension and alleviate pain associated with jaw clenching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which is common in cases of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
Calming and sedative properties: Maypop has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to jaw clenching or grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop plant contain a high concentration of compounds that help to relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension, making them effective for soothing jaw clenching.
Barks: The barks of the maypop plant have been used in traditional medicine to calm nervous energy and alleviate stress-related symptoms like jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 dried passiflora incarnata flowers and cut them into small pieces for processing.
Combine the chopped flowers with 8 ounces of filtered water in a clean glass jar and cover it with a lid.
Store the mixture in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours or overnight to allow for infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of steeping time.
Consume 1/2 cup of the decoction twice daily as needed, 15-20 minutes before meals to help relax the jaw muscles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with jaw clenching because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system. The valerenic acids present in valerian root have a sedative effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that often contribute to habitual jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation, valerian decoctions help to alleviate muscle tension in the face and jaw, releasing the physical strain associated with grinding or clenching teeth. This herbal remedy provides a soothing and calming influence, helping to restore balance to the nervous system and promote a sense of calm and tranquility.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative compounds, such as isovaleric acid and actinidine, help to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to reduced jaw tension and clenching.

Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing compounds, including valerenic acid, help to ease muscle spasms in the jaw, face, and neck, reducing discomfort and tension associated with jaw clenching.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Valerian's anxiolytic compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching and teeth grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in valepotriates, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to relax muscles and alleviate tension associated with jaw clenching.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of valerian contain a higher concentration of active compounds than the roots, making them particularly effective for treating muscle spasms and cramping, including those that can occur in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried valerian root and add it to a small saucepan.
Pour in two cups of boiling water over the valerian root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the decoction and mix well with a spoon.
Drink the warm decoction immediately after preparation and repeat as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with jaw clenching because it calms the nervous system, reducing tension and anxiety that often contribute to involuntary teeth grinding. The soothing properties of lavender relax the muscles in the face and neck, releasing physical stress and promoting a sense of tranquility. As a result, the habitual act of jaw clenching is disrupted, allowing for more relaxed oral habits and a reduction in discomfort or pain associated with teeth grinding.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant properties: English lavender contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which can help to relax tense muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing jaw clenching and tension headaches.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which often cause jaw clenching and grinding.
Calming and sedative properties: The calming and sedative compounds in lavender can promote relaxation and reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw clenching and teeth grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their calming and relaxing properties, which can help to reduce muscle tension in the jaw.
Buds: The buds are beneficial as they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can soothe jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and place it in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container to remove solids.
Discard the solids and add one teaspoon of honey to the decoction, stirring until dissolved.
Drink the decoction immediately, ideally three times daily for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi decoctions helps with jaw clenching because it effectively calms the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can lead to habitual teeth grinding. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to normalize bodily functions, including muscle tone, which eases tension in the jaw and facial muscles. Additionally, brahmi has a soothing effect on the mind, promoting relaxation and reducing mental fogginess, both of which can contribute to jaw clenching. By sipping on a warm brahmi decoction, individuals can experience relief from this common affliction.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: Brahmi's anxiolytic property helps to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant property: Brahmi's muscle relaxant property helps to soothe and relax the muscles of the face, neck, and jaw, reducing tension and spasms associated with jaw clenching.
Anti-inflammatory property: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory property may help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, Brahmi roots help reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw.
Rhyzomes: As a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, Brahmi rhyzomes soothe jaw pain and discomfort caused by clenching.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried bacopa monnieri powder into a small ceramic bowl.
Boil two cups of water in a saucepan over high heat for five minutes.
Add the measured bacopa monnieri powder to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes allowing the mixture to steep.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha decoctions helps with jaw clenching because it possesses adaptogenic properties that help regulate stress levels, which is a common cause of jaw clenching. When we experience anxiety or stress, our body's natural response is to tense up, leading to involuntary muscle contractions, including the masseter and temporalis muscles in the face, resulting in jaw clenching. Ashwagandha's ability to calm the nervous system and reduce cortisol levels can help alleviate this tension, allowing for a more relaxed jaw and reduced likelihood of clenching.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and stress-relieving: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching and teeth grinding.
Muscle relaxant and anti-spasmodic: Ashwagandha's muscle-relaxant properties can help ease tense muscles in the jaw and face, reducing clenching and grinding.

Inflammation-reducing and antioxidant-rich: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Ashwagandha roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and tension associated with jaw clenching.
Rhizomes: Containing a unique combination of alkaloids, rhizomes of Ashwagandha plant show promising results in reducing muscle spasms and relaxing the facial muscles.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ashwagandha root powder into a small bowl.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the powder in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup.
Drink the decoction warm or at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and soothing properties. The apigenin present in chamomile interacts with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. As a result, it can help alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular disorders (TMD) such as jaw clenching and grinding. By relaxing the jaw muscles, chamomile decoctions can reduce pain and discomfort associated with TMD, making it an effective natural remedy for managing this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and sedative effects: Chamomile's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant activity: Chamomile has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles of the face, including the masseter and temporalis muscles, which are responsible for jaw clenching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain soothing compounds that help relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.

Flowers: Chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, making them an effective ingredient for medicinal decoctions to alleviate jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in amounts of 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Heat 8 ounces of distilled water to a simmer in a saucepan over medium heat for 5-7 minutes.
Add the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to the hot water and steep for 10-15 minutes to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or bottle discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with jaw clenching because it relaxes the muscles in the face, neck, and shoulders that are often tensed when bruxism occurs. The menthol content in peppermint stimulates blood flow to these areas, reducing inflammation and discomfort. As the tension releases, the jaw muscles also relax, allowing the teeth to stop clenching. Additionally, the calming properties of peppermint promote a sense of calmness, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: Peppermint decoctions contain compounds like menthol, which helps to numb the jaw muscles, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with clenching.
Anti-inflammatory property: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in peppermint can reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw muscles and joints, easing tension and pain caused by clenching.

Muscle relaxant property: Peppermint's menthol and other compounds help to relax the jaw muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that contribute to clenching, allowing for a more comfortable and relaxed jaw.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to soothe and relax the jaw muscles, reducing tension and pain associated with jaw clenching.
Barks: Peppermint bark has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves into a heat-resistant glass cup.
Boil one cup of fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop and pour it over the peppermint leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, releasing its flavorful compounds and oils.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass cup, discarding solids.
Drink the warm peppermint tea immediately, taking small sips as needed to help relax jaw muscles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with jaw clenching because it possesses a unique blend of calming properties that soothe the nervous system. The herbal infusion contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to relax muscle contractions and reduce tension in the face and jaw area. Additionally, lemon balm's natural sedative effects help calm the mind and body, allowing for a reduction in subconscious teeth grinding and jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm muscle spasms in the jaw, reducing tension and clenching.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching and teeth grinding.
Sedative properties: Lemon balm's sedative properties can help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, making it easier to release tension in the jaw and surrounding muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds responsible for relaxing the muscles and reducing tension in the jaw.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and swelling associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the measuring cup and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Take 1/2 teaspoon of the cooled decoction three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap decoctions helps with jaw clenching because of its ability to calm the nervous system, which is often linked to teeth grinding and jaw tension. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in skullcap work together to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for bruxism. Additionally, skullcap's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate any underlying pain or discomfort that may be contributing to the clenching habit. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, skullcap decoctions provide a natural solution for managing jaw tension and associated symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Skullcap's antispasmodic effect helps to relax the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing tension and alleviating jaw clenching.
Anti-inflammatory property: Skullcap's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: Skullcap's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to relax muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds that soothe and calm the nerves, reducing jaw clenching caused by anxiety and stress.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried scutellaria lateriflora root in a clean glass cup.
Pour one cup of boiling filtered water over the herb and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into another container.
Discard the solids and use the liquid as directed, three times daily after meals.
Store any remaining decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw clenching with herbal decoctions is to combine Chamomile and Lavender flowers in a warm bath to relax the muscles. Valerian root tea can be consumed regularly to reduce muscle tension and anxiety, which often contribute to jaw clenching. Additionally, Ginger and Turmeric roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the affected area. Drink these decoctions thrice daily for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal decoctions are tension headaches, migraines, and facial pain. Herbs like feverfew, ginger, and willow bark are commonly used in decoctions to alleviate these conditions. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to relax tense muscles and reduce pain. The decoctions can be consumed orally or used as a topical application for added relief.
